context.user_request: when creating a configuration iam not able to see the nodes it says -forbidden

plan_item.summary: Task class: improve_docs — Add a troubleshooting entry for the 'forbidden' (HTTP 403) error in the Dynamic Configuration Manager docs, covering role and plan restrictions that prevent users from creating configurations or seeing nodes.
plan_item.edit_guidance:
In the existing ## Troubleshooting section (after the HTTP 412 subsection ending around line 294), add a new ### subsection titled something like 'Forbidden (HTTP 403) Error When Accessing Configuration Manager'. The entry should:

1. Quote the error message the user sees: 'forbidden' (HTTP 403) when attempting to create configurations or view nodes in the Configuration Manager.
2. Explain the two primary causes:
   a. **Insufficient role**: Only Admin and Manager roles can perform Dynamic Configuration actions (Add, Update, Enable/Disable, Remove, Test, View). Troubleshooters, Observers, and Billing roles will receive 'forbidden' for these actions. Reference the Role-Based Access Model docs.
   b. **Community (free) plan limitation**: A paid plan (Homelab, Business, or Enterprise) is required for all Dynamic Configuration Manager actions except 'List All'. Users on the Community plan will see 'forbidden' when attempting any action beyond listing.
3. Provide concrete resolution steps:
   - Check your assigned role in Space Settings → Users. If you are not an Admin or Manager, ask a Space Admin to upgrade your role.
   - Verify your subscription plan. If on Community, upgrade to a paid plan or ask a Space Admin to do so.
   - Confirm your Room assignment — users must be members of the Room containing the target nodes.
4. Cross-reference the RBAC docs at /docs/netdata-cloud/authentication-and-authorization/role-based-access-model.md for the full permissions table.

Follow the same format as the existing HTTP 412 subsection (use :::important and numbered resolution steps).
acceptance_checks: The new troubleshooting subsection appears under ## Troubleshooting in dynamic-configuration.md, after the existing HTTP 412 entry.
The entry quotes the exact error text 'forbidden' so users can match it via search.
Both root causes are covered: insufficient role (Troubleshooter/Observer/Billing) and Community plan limitation.
Resolution steps include checking role in Space Settings, verifying subscription plan, and confirming Room assignment.
A cross-reference link to the RBAC documentation is included.
No changes to the RBAC doc itself or other files — this is a single-target surgical edit.
The new entry follows the same formatting pattern as the existing HTTP 412 troubleshooting subsection.

context.diff:
commit 211914432845e855c08e525270068b9e2a1a1515
Author: Stelios Fragkakis <52996999+stelfrag@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Jun 8 10:59:50 2026 +0300

    Fix sum on incremental dimensions for archived dims (#22580)
    
    fix sum on incremental dimensions for archived dims
    
      query_target.c read the algorithm from the live RRDDIM, so SUM grouping on
      incremental dims whose RRDDIM was no longer in memory (host disconnected,
      chart obsoleted, parent-side child history) skipped the rate->volume
      scaling and returned raw rate sums, off by source_update_every.
    
      Persist RRD_ALGORITHM on RRDMETRIC, populate it from the live RRDDIM, the
      SQL dimension row at startup, and rrddim_set_algorithm at runtime. Switch
      query_target.c to read it atomically from RRDMETRIC, no live RRDDIM
      required.
    
      No schema migration; the dimension.algorithm column already exists.
---
